response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang credentials, but the role demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, Spring ecosystem proficiency, and IDE internals knowledge — none of which are meaningfully present in this resume. Java appears only in the skills list and briefly in early-career roles; Kotlin is absent entirely. The candidate's work at National Instruments involved writing C/C++ components for an IDE rather than building IDE features, and there is no evidence of JVM tooling, Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience anywhere in a 25-year career.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) consistently applied across multiple roles
Demonstrated ability to build and maintain complex, large-scale systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic)
Early IDE-adjacent exposure at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE, plus a history of developer tooling projects
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin expertise; primary languages throughout career are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
Zero experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework
No IDE internals or language tooling experience from the JVM ecosystem; existing developer tooling work is in non-JVM stacks
